title: Lesson 16 — Feast or Famine

words.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in daily life.

words.definition: spreading widely throughout an area or a group
words.example: A pervasive smell of smoke clung to everything.

words.definition: widespread; existing commonly at a particular time or place
words.example: The belief was prevalent throughout the region.
Myriad stars filled the clear desert sky.
Copious notes filled three thick notebooks.

words.definition: plentiful; produced or given in large amounts
words.example: Profuse apologies followed the embarrassing mistake.

words.definition: an excess or overabundance of something
words.example: A plethora of options left the buyer paralyzed.

words.definition: a scarcity or lack of something
words.example: A dearth of rain ruined the summer harvest.

words.definition: the presence of something in only small or insufficient quantities
words.example: A paucity of hard evidence weakened the case.
Sparse applause greeted the closing speech.

words.definition: distant from and superior to ordinary life; select; (of air) thin
words.example: The rarefied world of elite art auctions.
They paid scant attention to the storm warning.

story: On the coast, wealth was [[ubiquitous|ubiquitous]] and optimism [[pervasive|pervasive]]; the boom grew so [[prevalent|prevalent]] that [[myriad|myriad]] newcomers arrived weekly, greeted by [[copious|copious]] jobs and [[profuse|profuse]] promises — a genuine [[plethora|plethora]] of opportunity. Inland told the opposite story. A [[dearth|dearth]] of rain and a [[paucity|paucity]] of work left [[sparse|sparse]] crowds in the once-busy square. Only a [[rarefied|rarefied]] few still prospered, while the rest scraped by on [[scant|scant]] wages and even thinner hope.
